rulings.Case Title: CATHERAL HILL TOWER CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION VS. LARISA GARBAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 25, 2007, LINE 2. CROSS-DEFENDANT CATHEDRAL HILL TOWER CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED AN ABILITY TO PLEAD FACTS REFLECTING CROSS-DEFENDANTS FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE STANDARD IN LAMDEN V LAJOLLA SHORES CLUBDOMINIUM HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION (1999) 21 CAL 4TH 249. THE COURT OF APPEALS ALREADY HAS HELD THAT THE FACTS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPH 20 OF THE THIRD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT DO NOT MEET THE LAMDEN STANDARD. THE ADDITIONAL FACTS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPH 16 DO NOT DIFFER MATERIALLY FROM THE FACTS IN PARAGRAPH 20 OF THE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. FOR THAT REASON, THE DEMURRER, ONCE AGAIN, IS SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: CANDACE WATERS VS. JEANNE WOODFORD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 25, 2007, LINE 5. PLAINTIFF CANDACE WATERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied because plaintiff has failed to shift the burden of proof and/or there are triable issues of fact raised by defendants. REMAINDER OF THE TENTATIVE RULING IS AVAILABLE ON THE REGISTER OF ACTION THROUGH THE COURT'S MINUTES FOR JULY 24, 2007. A COPY OF THE TENTATIVE RULING WILL BE FAXED TO KATHRYN PALAMOUNTAIN (CHAVEZ & GERTLER) AND DOUGLAS WOODS (ATTORNEY GENERAL'S OFFICE). =(302/PJM/DS-X)

rulings.Case Title: RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEES' VS. LYDIA KASHEVAROFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 25, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANT LIDYA KASHEVAROFF'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANT WAS A FIDUCIARY TO PLAINTIFF. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER C.C.P SECTION 339, A TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. TWENTY DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT TO TOLL THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES DEFENDANT'S DUTY TO DISCLOSE AND NO AUTHORITY HAS BEEN CITED TO SUPPORT THE ASSERTION THAT PLAINTIFF HAD A DUTY TO INVESTIGATE. THE QUASI CONTRACT CLAIM BASED ON FRAUD IS GOVERNED BY C.C.P. SECTION 338 AND IS FOR THREE YEARS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA WILLIAMS VS. JULIE YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 25, 2007, LINE 7 DEFENDANTS JULIE YEE AND DAVID YEE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS ESTABLISHING A BASIS FOR A CONTINUING NUISANCE. PLAINTIFF'S FACTS ALL RELATE TO THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE PATIO IN 1993 (PARAGRAPHS 7 AND 8). PARAGRAPH 14 IS A LEGAL CONCLUSION, NOT A STATEMENT OF FACT. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: ATR-KIM ENG FINANCIAL CORPORATION VS. CARLOS ARANETA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action; Memo Of P And A
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 25, 2007, LINE 14. DEFENDANTS SANTIAGO ARANETA, JUAN ARANETA, FERNANDO ARANETA AND MONICA ARANETA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action: MOTION TO QUASH IS CONTINUED FOR SIXTY DAYS TO SEPTEMBER 24, 2007 AT 9:30 A. M. IN THE INTERIM, THE PARTIES ARE TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY TO ASCERTAIN DEFENDANTS' KNOWLEDGE OF THE REASON FOR THE STOCK TRANSFER AND THEIR INVOLVEMENT IN THE TRANSFER. MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY IS DENIED FOR THE REASONS STATED IN CONNECTION WITH CARLOS ARANETA'S MOTION TO DISMISS/STAY. =(302/PJM/DS-X)

rulings.Case Title: ATR-KIM ENG FINANCIAL CORPORATION VS. CARLOS ARANETA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons And Mtn To Dismiss Or Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 25, 2007, LINE 14. DEFENDANT CARLOS ARANETA'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons And MOTION To Dismiss Or Stay Action IS DENIED BASED ON CALDER V JONES (1984) 465 U.S. 783. THE TRANSFERS OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT WAS PRESENT DURING THE JANUARY TRANSACTION AND OWNS STOCK IN A CALIFORNIA COMPANY. THE ALLEGED PURPOSE OF DEFENDANT'S TRANSFER WAS TO PRECLUDE PLAINTIFF FROM COLLECTING A JUDGMENT IN CALIFORNIA. THE MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY IS DENIED. THE CENTRAL EVENTS AT ISSUE OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA AND CALIFORNIA HAS AN INTEREST IN SEEING THAT ITS LAWS ARE ENFORCED. GIVEN THAT ALL PARTIES RESIDE IN THE PHILLIPINES, THERE IS NO MORE HARDSHIP ON DEFENDANT THAN PLAINTIFF. =(302/PJM/DS-X)

rulings.Case Title: MAUREEN SCHMIDT VS. LORGIA GUTIERREZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Rene Colomer, Lorgia Gutierrez, And Hazel Colomer'S Motion To Seal The Record Pertaining To Case No. Cud 06-618423
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JULY 25, 2007, LINE 19. DEFENDANTS RENE COLOMER, LORGIA GUTIERREZ AND HAZEL COLOMER'S MOTION TO SEAL RECORD IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS DID NOT PREVAIL WITHIN SIXTY DAYS AS REQUIRED UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 1161.2 AND THERE IS NO BASIS TO SEAL THE RECORDS UNDER C.R.C. SECTION 2.551. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MET THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.R.C. SECTION 2.550 TO SEAL THE RECORDS. C.C. SECTION 1786.18 (A) (4) IS FOR THE BENEFIT OF DEFENDANTS IN ADDRESSING CREDIT REPORTING ISSUES. =(302/PJM/CK)
